_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to analyse how authors organise ideas to develop and shape the meaning of their texts.

_source.w: We are learning to analyse how authors organise ideas to develop and shape the meaning of their texts.
y: Understanding the organisation of ideas reveals an author's strategy for building their argument or narrative.
This analytical skill helps us read complex texts efficiently and evaluate their effectiveness.
Applying this understanding to our own writing makes us more strategic and purposeful authors.
eg: We map the organisational structure of an essay and explain how each section builds the argument.
We analyse how a narrative's structure — chronological, flashback, parallel — shapes meaning.
We identify how an author sequences ideas to build towards a conclusion or climax.
We evaluate whether a text's organisation effectively supports its purpose.
We compare different organisational approaches to the same topic and assess their effectiveness.
We plan our own extended texts by deliberately choosing an organisational structure that serves our purpose.
